For the front-end portion we used HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and for the back-end portion we used MongoDB, Express Js and Node Js.

-- 4 of 14 -- 4 ABSTRACT This is a full-stack Tour Planning website. For the front-end portion we used HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and for the back-end portion we used MongoDB, Express Js and Node Js. There are significant number of companies that provides real-time travel information, prices and availability for flights, trains, buses, and hotels, and allows ticket booking through its associate website and apps but no neither of them has the feature or 8Planning a Tour9. We discovered this sticky situation and conceived the idea of making this project which allows users to plan a tour. Here we provide a list of tours from which a person can select according to their aspiration.

Here we provide a list of tours from which a person can select according to their aspiration. There is assortment of tours for a wanderer like Forest Hiker, Park Camper, Sea Explorer, Snow Adventurer and Sports Lover. The user information during the Signup / Login or password is stored at back-end. Additional features for users are that they can rate on tours, can upload images in review box plus they do credit card payments as well. Bibliography 12 Downloaded by sintayehu mulugeta (sentaman20@gmail.com) lOMoARcPSD|60467436 -- 7 of 14 -- 7 INTRODUCTION Project NATOURS as the name suggests is the combination of nature and tours (NATURE + TOURS). It is a website where anyone can book a full tour of the place that he/she wants to visit. Here, we also provide feedbacks where people can post their feedback regarding the tour. This website was made using React JS, Express JS, Mongo DB and NodeJS.

Here, we also provide feedbacks where people can post their feedback regarding the tour. This website was made using React JS, Express JS, Mongo DB and NodeJS. A person can login and can keep a tour in their wish list if they want and can plan a tour anytime in the future to that location. You can also add credit or debit card for purchases and get huge discounts. The website has minimalistic UI which makes the website more user friendly and easy to navigate. The website aims to get the best possible deals for planning a tour without having to waste time in unnecessary searching and hassle. It makes easy for a person to plan a tour. FLOWCHART Downloaded by sintayehu mulugeta (sentaman20@gmail.com) lOMoARcPSD|60467436 -- 9 of 14 -- 9 FEASIBILITY STUDY/ VERIFICATION & VALIDATION For the verification and validation of this project, a user was first created who first signed his id.

-- 9 of 14 -- 9 FEASIBILITY STUDY/ VERIFICATION & VALIDATION For the verification and validation of this project, a user was first created who first signed his id. Then it was checked whether its data was stored in the database or not. After signup, the login feature was checked. First by entering the wrong password in it, then by entering the correct password. After signup login, checked the features of the account page like image uploads, and contact verification. In this website, the user can buy any tour and give rating to that tour package. And this website shows this rating by calculating average rating, so this feature was also checked by making 10-15 accounts. After taking complete information about the tour package, the user can also buy it with his credit card.

After taking complete information about the tour package, the user can also buy it with his credit card. As soon as the user buys any tour and buys it with a credit card, his data gets stored on Stripe. With the help of Stripe, we have enabled the payment gateway in this project. As soon as a user purchases, his data will be stored on Stripe, as well as this tour package will also be uploaded on the user's My Package page. If both these things are done well then this feature of ours is working properly and these things are done well. After this, the normal features were checked with the help of the user we had created. The backbone of this website is node.js which was the most important thing to check.

After this, the normal features were checked with the help of the user we had created. The backbone of this website is node.js which was the most important thing to check. Node.js takes the data from the database and dumps the data into the HTML structure dynamically. To check this we created a new tour package.In which we gave a little information about the tour to the node and nodejs gave us a complete tour package. This is how we verified and validated our entire project. Downloaded by sintayehu mulugeta (sentaman20@gmail.com) lOMoARcPSD|60467436 -- 10 of 14 -- 10 SOFTWARE/ HARDWARE REQUIREMENTS 1.

SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS: Languages Used: HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript, Node Js, Express Js, MongoDB Client-side Environment: Web Browsers (Chrome, Internet Explorer 6.0 or above etc.) Server Environments: MySQL 15.1, Apache Server 2.4.52 2. HARDWARE REQUIREMENTS: Processor: 1.9 GHz x86- or x64- bit dual core processor Memory (RAM): 2- GB RAM Display: VGA display of Resolution 1920x1080 Downloaded by sintayehu mulugeta (sentaman20@gmail.com) lOMoARcPSD|60467436 -- 11 of 14 -- 11 TECHNOLOGIES USED HTML: HTML stands for Hypertext Markup Language. It is used to design web pages using a markup language.

-- 11 of 14 -- 11 TECHNOLOGIES USED HTML: HTML stands for Hypertext Markup Language. It is used to design web pages using a markup language. HTML is the combination of Hypertext and Markup language. Hypertext defines the link between the web pages. A markup language is used to define the text document within tag which defines the structure of web pages. CSS: CSS stands for Cascading Style Sheets. It is the language for describing the presentation of Web pages, including colours, layout, and fonts, thus making our web pages presentable to the users. CSS is designed to make style sheets for the web. It is independent of HTML and can be used with any XML-based markup guage.

CSS is designed to make style sheets for the web. It is independent of HTML and can be used with any XML-based markup guage. JavaScript: JavaScript (js) is a light-weight object-oriented programming language which is used by several websites for scripting the webpages. It is an interpreted, full- fledged programming language that enables dynamic interactivity on websites when applied to an HTML document. It was introduced in the year 1995 for adding programs to the webpages in the Netscape Navigator browser. Since then, it has been adopted by all other graphical web browsers. With JavaScript, users can build modern web applications to interact directly without reloading the page every time. The traditional website uses js to provide several forms of interactivity and simplicity.

The traditional website uses js to provide several forms of interactivity and simplicity. Express Js: Express.js, or simply Express, is a back end web application framework for Node.js, released as free and open-source software under the MIT License. It is designed for building web applications and APIs. It has been called the de facto standard server framework for Node.js. MongoDB: MongoDB is an open-source document database and leading NoSQL database. MongoDB is written in C++. This tutorial will give you great understanding on MongoDB concepts needed to create and deploy a highly scalable and performance- oriented database. Node.js: Node.js is an open-source and cross-platform JavaScript runtime environment. It is a popular tool for almost any kind of project!

Node.js: Node.js is an open-source and cross-platform JavaScript runtime environment. It is a popular tool for almost any kind of project! Node.js runs the V8 JavaScript engine, the core of Google Chrome, outside of the browser. This allows Node.js to be very performant. A Node.js app runs in a single process, without creating a new thread for every request. Node.js provides a set of asynchronous I/O primitives in its standard library that prevent JavaScript code from blocking and generally, libraries in Node.js are written using non-blocking paradigms, making blocking behavior the exception rather than the norm Downloaded by sintayehu mulugeta (sentaman20@gmail.com) lOMoARcPSD|60467436 -- 12 of 14 --

-- 11 of 14 -- 11 TECHNOLOGIES USED HTML: HTML stands for Hypertext Markup Language. It is used to design web pages using a markup language. HTML is the combination of Hypertext and Markup language. Hypertext defines the link between the web pages. A markup language is used to define the text document within tag which defines the structure of web pages. CSS: CSS stands for Cascading Style Sheets. It is the language for describing the presentation of Web pages, including colours, layout, and fonts, thus making our web pages presentable to the users. CSS is designed to make style sheets for the web. It is independent of HTML and can be used with any XML-based markup guage.

CSS is designed to make style sheets for the web. It is independent of HTML and can be used with any XML-based markup guage. JavaScript: JavaScript (js) is a light-weight object-oriented programming language which is used by several websites for scripting the webpages. It is an interpreted, full- fledged programming language that enables dynamic interactivity on websites when applied to an HTML document. It was introduced in the year 1995 for adding programs to the webpages in the Netscape Navigator browser. Since then, it has been adopted by all other graphical web browsers. With JavaScript, users can build modern web applications to interact directly without reloading the page every time. The traditional website uses js to provide several forms of interactivity and simplicity.

The traditional website uses js to provide several forms of interactivity and simplicity. Express Js: Express.js, or simply Express, is a back end web application framework for Node.js, released as free and open-source software under the MIT License. It is designed for building web applications and APIs. It has been called the de facto standard server framework for Node.js. MongoDB: MongoDB is an open-source document database and leading NoSQL database. MongoDB is written in C++. This tutorial will give you great understanding on MongoDB concepts needed to create and deploy a highly scalable and performance- oriented database. Node.js: Node.js is an open-source and cross-platform JavaScript runtime environment. It is a popular tool for almost any kind of project!

Node.js: Node.js is an open-source and cross-platform JavaScript runtime environment. It is a popular tool for almost any kind of project! Node.js runs the V8 JavaScript engine, the core of Google Chrome, outside of the browser. This allows Node.js to be very performant. A Node.js app runs in a single process, without creating a new thread for every request. Node.js provides a set of asynchronous I/O primitives in its standard library that prevent JavaScript code from blocking and generally, libraries in Node.js are written using non-blocking paradigms, making blocking behavior the exception rather than the norm Downloaded by sintayehu mulugeta (sentaman20@gmail.com) lOMoARcPSD|60467436 -- 12 of 14 --
